title: Tamil morpheme 'ney' (ghee) in classical literature
description: Tamil Sangam literature uses 'ney' for ghee; attested in Tholkaappiyam grammar.

title: Tamil 'aa' as morpheme for cow in Tholkaappiyam
description: Tholkaappiyam establishes 'aa' (ஆ) as ancient morpheme for cow, predating written script.

title: Absence of military imagery in Harappan sites
description: No weapons, no battle scenes, no depictions of military hierarchy found in 100 years of excavation.
source_citation: Kenoyer J.M. (1998). Ancient Cities of the Indus Valley Civilization.

title: Evidence of cattle pens near seal deposits
description: Structural remains of cattle enclosures found near seal concentrations in Mohenjo-daro.
source_citation: Mackay E.J.H. (1938). Further Excavations at Mohenjo-daro.

title: Rao et al. (2009) entropy analysis
description: Indus signs show conditional entropy values consistent with natural languages, inconsistent with random or simple code systems.
source_citation: Rao R. et al. (2009). Entropic Evidence for Linguistic Structure in the Indus Script. Science 324:1165.

title: Graffiti marks on Tamil Nadu pottery
description: Indus signs and graffiti marks of Tamil Nadu show morphological continuity.
source_citation: K.Rajan, R.Sivamantham. Indus Signs and Graffiti Marks of Tamil Nadu. Dept of Archaeology, Govt of Tamil Nadu. 2025.

title: Loanwords in early Indo-Aryan supporting Dravidian substrate
description: First Indo-Aryan writings contain Dravidian loanwords suggesting a pre-existing Dravidian-speaking substrate population.
source_citation: Witzel M. (1999). Substrate Languages in Old Indo-Aryan. EJVS 5(1).

title: Standardised weights and measures across 1.3M sq km
description: Uniform binary weight system found across all major sites suggests centralised administration requiring written records.
source_citation: Kenoyer J.M. (1994). Interaction Systems, Specialised Crafts and Culture Change. In The Indo-Aryans of Ancient South Asia.
